text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little village nestled between green hills and clear rivers lived a curious fox named Felix. Felix was an unusual fox. Instead of floppy ears like his kin, he had razor-sharp claws that gleamed crimson in the moonlight. And instead of tails, Felix wore tiny brass circles. Each evening, Felix liked to wander the outskirts of town. There were secrets he sought among the ancient oaks.

One twilight, Felix stumbled upon an old oak tree that seemed almost crying. It bowed preciously towards Felix as if remembering things forgotten. Something twitched inside Felix’s crimson coat. He ventured closer.

“Mr. Oak,” Felix whispered. “What seems wrong?”

The tree leaned more—a great swoop down.

“Oh! You know.”

There, hidden amidst the leaves, was a tiny lady bird. Her feathers were a faded shade of apricot. “You’re weary,” Felix concluded. “Tell me a secret.”

The lady bird sighed.

“You’re right.
